title: Maximum Sum Subarray of Size K
description: Given an integer array `views` and an integer `k`, return the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray of exactly `k` elements. If `k` is greater than `views.length`, return `0`.

examples.explanation: Window [9,2,6] sums to 17, the highest 3-window sum.

hints: Pehle k elements ka sum nikal le, fir window slide kar
Slide karte time naya add, purana minus
Edge: k > n hua to 0 return kar

title: Number of Sub-arrays of Size K With Average Below Threshold
description: Given an integer array `times`, an integer `k`, and an integer `threshold`, return the number of contiguous subarrays of size exactly `k` whose average is strictly less than `threshold`. If `k` is greater than `times.length`, return `0`.
hints: Avg compare karne ke bajaye sum vs k*threshold compare kar
Window sum slide kar k size ka
Strictly less than condition mein equal count nahi hota

title: Window Sum Exceeds Limit
description: Given an integer array `txns` and integers `k` and `limit`, return `true` if any contiguous subarray of size `k` has a sum strictly greater than `limit`, and `false` otherwise. If `k` is greater than `txns.length`, return `false`.
hints: Standard fixed window — bas early return kar de jab cross ho
Strict greater than dhyan se
k zyada ho to false

title: Maximum Number of Vowels in a Substring of Given Length
description: Given a lowercase string `s` and an integer `k`, return the maximum number of vowels (`a`, `e`, `i`, `o`, `u`) contained in any contiguous substring of length `k`. If `k` is greater than `s.length`, return `0`.
hints: Vowel set use kar fast lookup ke liye
Window slide kar — entering vowel +1, leaving vowel -1
Initial window pehle compute karna na bhul

title: Minimum Sum Subarray of Size K
description: Given an integer array `loads` and an integer `k`, return the minimum sum of any contiguous subarray of exactly `k` elements. If `k` is greater than `loads.length`, return `-1`.
hints: Max ki jagah min track kar
Standard fixed window template
k > n hua to -1

title: Max Consecutive Ones
description: Given a binary array `status` consisting only of `0`s and `1`s, return the length of the longest contiguous subarray that contains only `1`s.
hints: Variable window — left fix, right expand jab tak 1 mile
0 dikha to left = right + 1 kar de
Empty array case sambhal

title: Distinct Elements in First Window
description: Given an integer array `views` and an integer `k`, return the number of distinct values in the first contiguous window of size exactly `k` (that is, the first `k` elements). If `k` is greater than `views.length`, return `0`.
hints: Sirf first window — Set use kar
k > n ho to 0
Set ka size return kar

title: Fixed-Size Window Sums
description: Given an integer array `points` and an integer `k`, return an array in which the element at index `i` is the sum of the elements `points[i..i+k-1]`. The returned array has length `points.length - k + 1`. If `k` is greater than `points.length`, return an empty array.
hints: Sare windows ke sums chahiye — array banao
Slide kar O(n) mein
k > n hua to khali array

title: Minimum Window Substring
description: Given a string `requests` and a string `needed`, return the length of the smallest contiguous substring of `requests` that contains every character of `needed`, including duplicates (each required character must appear at least as many times as it does in `needed`). If no such substring exists, return `0`.
hints: Variable window: right expand, fir left contract jab valid ho
HashMap mein needed counts rakh, fir matched counter maintain kar
Min length update karte raho jab valid ho

title: Longest Substring with At Most K Distinct Characters
description: Given a string `keys` and an integer `k`, return the length of the longest contiguous substring that contains at most `k` distinct characters. If `keys` is empty or `k` is `0`, the answer is `0`.
hints: Variable window with HashMap of char freq
Distinct count > k ho to left contract kar
Empty string ya k=0 edge case

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
description: Given an integer array `skus`, return the length of the longest contiguous subarray in which every value is distinct (contains no repeated values).
hints: HashMap me last seen index store kar
Duplicate dikhne par left ko Math.max(left, lastSeen+1) pe le ja
Empty array case

title: Sliding Window Maximum
description: Given an integer array `nums` and an integer `k`, return an array in which the element at index `i` is the maximum of the contiguous window `nums[i..i+k-1]`. The returned array has length `nums.length - k + 1`. If `k` is greater than `nums.length`, return an empty array.
hints: Monotonic decreasing deque of indices use kar
Front index window se bahar nikla to pop
Naya element add karne se pehle peeche se chote saare nikal

title: Sliding Window Minimum
description: Given an integer array `cpc` and an integer `k`, return an array in which the element at index `i` is the minimum of the contiguous window `cpc[i..i+k-1]`. The returned array has length `cpc.length - k + 1`. If `k` is greater than `cpc.length`, return an empty array.
hints: Monotonic INCREASING deque
Same logic as max but reverse comparison
Front always points to current min

title: Find All Anagrams in a String
description: Given two lowercase strings `text` and `pat`, return the number of contiguous substrings of `text` that are anagrams of `pat`. Two strings are anagrams if one is a permutation of the other.
hints: Fixed window length = pat.length
Char freq array of size 26
Compare arrays cleverly using matched counter

title: Max Consecutive Ones III
description: Given a binary array `log` consisting of `0`s and `1`s and an integer `k`, you may flip at most `k` values from `0` to `1`. Return the length of the longest contiguous subarray containing only `1`s that can be obtained after performing at most `k` flips.

examples.explanation: Flip 2 zeros in the right stretch to get 7 consecutive 1s.

hints: Variable window: zeros count <= k allowed
Right expand, left contract jab zeros > k
Best window length track kar

title: Subarray Product Less Than K
description: Given an array of positive integers `costs` and an integer `k`, return the number of contiguous non-empty subarrays whose product of all elements is strictly less than `k`.
hints: Variable window with running product
Product >= k ho to left badha aur divide kar
Har valid right pe (r - l + 1) subarrays add ho

title: Minimum Size Subarray Sum
description: Given an array of positive integers `orders` and an integer `S`, return the length of the shortest contiguous subarray whose sum is greater than or equal to `S`. If no such subarray exists, return `0`.
hints: Variable window: expand jab sum < S, contract jab sum >= S
Min length record karte raho
Positive only — monotonic

title: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
description: Given an uppercase string `log` and an integer `k`, you may replace at most `k` characters with any uppercase English letter. Return the length of the longest contiguous substring that, after at most `k` replacements, consists of a single repeating character.

examples.explanation: Replace one B → 'AABA'+'A' chunk yields 'AAAA' window length 4.

hints: Window mein max-freq char track kar
Window length - maxFreq <= k valid hai
Violation ho to left aage

title: Count Requests in Time Window
description: Given a non-decreasing array `timestamps` of request times (in milliseconds) and an integer `windowMs`, return an array `counts` where `counts[i]` is the number of requests that fall within the half-open window `(timestamps[i] - windowMs, timestamps[i]]`.

examples.explanation: For t=10, window is (5,10], includes 10 — count 1. Wait recompute: t=10, window (5,10] includes only 10 → 1. Actual answer differs. Recompute strictly: t=1 (-4,1] → {1}=1. t=2 (-3,2] → {1,2}=2. t=5 (0,5] → {1,2,5}=3. t=10 (5,10] → {10}=1. t=11 (6,11] → {10,11}=2. So output is [1,2,3,1,2].

hints: Two pointers — left aage badha jab tak ts[left] <= ts[r] - windowMs
Half-open window (ts[r] - windowMs, ts[r]]
Counts are r - left + 1

title: Count Distinct Elements in Every Window of Size K
description: Given an integer array `merchants` and an integer `k`, return an array in which the element at index `i` is the number of distinct values in the contiguous window `merchants[i..i+k-1]`. The returned array has length `merchants.length - k + 1`. If `k` is greater than `merchants.length`, return an empty array.
hints: Frequency map maintain kar
Add new element, remove leftmost when window size > k
Distinct count = map.size jab freq>0

title: Longest Window Containing All Values
description: Given an integer array `ratings` whose values are each between `1` and `5` inclusive, return the length of the longest contiguous subarray in which every value from `1` to `5` appears at least once. If no such subarray exists, return `0`.

examples.explanation: Smallest cover is [1..5], the only valid window length is 5.

hints: Find SMALLEST window covering 1-5, then... wait, problem says LONGEST
Longest window covering all 5 — basically suffix-prefix
Track first occurrence and last occurrence?

title: Subarrays with K Different Integers
description: Given an integer array `events` and an integer `k`, return the number of contiguous subarrays that contain exactly `k` distinct values.
hints: Trick: exactly K = atMost(K) - atMost(K-1)
atMost(K) classic sliding window pattern
Cumulative count ko subarray-ending-at-r se add kar

title: Minimum Window Substring II
description: Given two lowercase strings `s` and `t`, return the smallest substring of `s` that contains every character of `t`, including duplicates. If multiple substrings share the smallest length, return the leftmost one. If no such substring exists, return an empty string.

examples.explanation: 'banc' is the leftmost smallest containing a, b, c.

hints: Need-freq map se start kar
Window expand, fir contract jab valid
Best (left, right) update karo only if strictly smaller — leftmost banaye rakhne ke liye

title: Contiguous Array
description: Given a binary array `seats` consisting of `0`s and `1`s, return the length of the longest contiguous subarray that contains an equal number of `0`s and `1`s.
hints: 0 ko -1 treat kar, fir prefix sum 0 wala longest subarray dhundh
Pehli baar dikhne wala prefix sum index store kar
Pure sliding window nahi — prefix sum + hashmap (extended sliding window pattern)

title: Subarray Sum Equals K
description: Given an integer array `scores`, which may contain negative values, and an integer `T`, return the number of contiguous non-empty subarrays whose sum is exactly `T`.
hints: Negatives hain to pure sliding window nahi — prefix sum + hashmap
Har prefix sum ki freq store kar
Current prefix sum - T pehle aaya tha to count += freq
